headline: Apple (AAPL) Teams Up With Intel To Build Chips In The United States
summary: Apple and Intel are partnering to design and manufacture chips in the United States, with direct backing from the U.S. administration. The move is intended to address memory supply disruptions and price pressure linked to AI driven chip demand. The partnership aligns with broader government efforts to build domestic semiconductor capacity and reduce reliance on overseas manufacturing. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:AAPL, this shift comes as Apple trades at $298.01 per share, with the stock...

headline: 2 High-Flying Stocks to Research Further and 1 That Underwhelm
